text:
DEPARTMENT OF PATHOLOGY AND LABORATORY MEDICINE
SURGICAL PATHOLOGY REPORT

PATIENT: Raymond Okafor
MRN: [REDACTED]
DOB: [REDACTED]
SEX: Male
ORDERING PHYSICIAN: Gastroenterology / Oncology
CLINICAL SERVICE: Gastroenterology (Colonoscopy Suite) / Interventional Radiology
ACCESSION NUMBER: SP-[REDACTED]
PRIORITY: ROUTINE / STAT (Dual Specimen)

---

CLINICAL HISTORY:
A 58-year-old male presenting with a several-month history of altered bowel habits, intermittent hematochezia, and unintentional weight loss of approximately 12 pounds over the preceding 4 months. Colonoscopy identified a circumferential, partially obstructing mass in the sigmoid colon at approximately 28 cm from the anal verge. CT staging demonstrated the sigmoid mass (4.8 cm) associated with pericolic fat stranding, multiple enlarged mesenteric lymph nodes, and multiple bilobar hypodense hepatic lesions (largest 3.2 cm in segment VII). CEA at presentation: 187.3 ng/mL. CA 19-9: 412 U/mL. Specimens submitted: (A) colonoscopic biopsy of sigmoid colon mass and (B) CT-guided core needle biopsy of hepatic segment VII lesion performed concurrently by interventional radiology.

---

SPECIMENS SUBMITTED:

Part A: SIGMOID COLON MASS, COLONOSCOPIC BIOPSY
  - Received in formalin: multiple irregular tan-pink tissue fragments
  - Specimen dimensions: aggregate 1.2 x 0.9 x 0.4 cm
  - Number of fragments: 6
  - Clinical designation: "Sigmoid mass, biopsy at 28 cm"

Part B: HEPATIC SEGMENT VII LESION, CT-GUIDED CORE NEEDLE BIOPSY
  - Received in formalin: two tan-white core needle biopsy cores
  - Core dimensions: 1.5 cm x 0.1 cm and 1.3 cm x 0.1 cm
  - Clinical designation: "Liver segment VII lesion, CT-guided core biopsy"

---

GROSS DESCRIPTION:

Part A -- Sigmoid Colon Mass, Colonoscopic Biopsy:
Received in formalin, labeled with the patient's name, medical record number, and site designation "sigmoid mass biopsy at 28 cm," are six irregular tan-pink to focally hemorrhagic tissue fragments with a combined aggregate dimension of 1.2 x 0.9 x 0.4 cm. The tissue fragments are soft to focally firm in consistency. The cut surfaces are tan-pink and homogeneous. No grossly necrotic or cystic areas identified. All tissue submitted entirely in one cassette (A1) after routine formalin fixation.

Part B -- Hepatic Segment VII Lesion, CT-Guided Core Needle Biopsy:
Received in formalin, labeled with the patient's name, medical record number, and site designation "liver segment VII lesion," are two core needle biopsy cores. Core 1 measures 1.5 cm in length x 0.1 cm in diameter; core 2 measures 1.3 cm in length x 0.1 cm in diameter. Both cores are tan-white and firm, with focal areas of pale yellow discoloration and mild hemorrhage. The consistency of both cores is firmer than expected for normal hepatic parenchyma, consistent with fibrotic or neoplastic tissue. Entirely submitted in one cassette (B1) after routine formalin fixation.

---

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:

Part A -- Sigmoid Colon Mass, Colonoscopic Biopsy:
Histologic sections of the sigmoid colon biopsy demonstrate fragments of colonic mucosa and submucosa infiltrated by a malignant epithelial neoplasm. The tumor is composed of irregular, angulated, and back-to-back glandular structures with marked architectural distortion. The glands are lined by columnar to cuboidal cells with moderate to abundant amphophilic cytoplasm. Nuclear features include moderate to marked pleomorphism, prominent nucleoli, increased nuclear-to-cytoplasmic ratio, and coarse chromatin. Mitotic figures are readily identified, including atypical forms. Intraluminal dirty necrosis (comedonecrosis) is present within many of the neoplastic glands, a characteristic finding of colorectal-type adenocarcinoma. A desmoplastic stromal response is present. The muscularis mucosae is disrupted, and tumor infiltrates into the submucosa in sampled fragments. There is no squamous differentiation, signet ring cell change, or mucinous component identified in these biopsy fragments (mucinous component comprising less than 50% of the tumor). Moderate acute and chronic inflammatory infiltrate is present in the adjacent stroma. Background colonic mucosa shows areas of reactive change.

Histologic grade: MODERATELY DIFFERENTIATED (Grade 2) per WHO classification (irregular tubular architecture with readily identifiable gland formation comprising the majority of the tumor).

Part B -- Hepatic Segment VII Lesion, CT-Guided Core Needle Biopsy:
Histologic sections of the hepatic core biopsy demonstrate cores of tissue composed predominantly of metastatic carcinoma with a minor component of compressed, non-neoplastic hepatic parenchyma at the periphery of one core. The neoplasm is architecturally and cytologically identical to the primary sigmoid colon lesion: irregular, back-to-back malignant glands with intraluminal dirty necrosis, a desmoplastic stroma, and columnar cells with moderate nuclear pleomorphism and prominent nucleoli. No hepatocellular carcinoma pattern (trabecular, acinar) is identified. The residual hepatic parenchyma shows mild macro- and microvesicular steatosis consistent with background fatty liver change; no significant fibrosis is appreciated in the non-neoplastic liver on limited sampling. Portal tracts are not well-represented in the biopsy cores.

---

ANCILLARY STUDIES:

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY (IHC) -- Part A (Sigmoid Colon Mass) and Part B (Hepatic Core Biopsy):

MMR Protein Expression (Part A -- Sigmoid Colon Mass):
  - MLH1: INTACT nuclear expression (no loss of expression)
  - MSH2: INTACT nuclear expression (no loss of expression)
  - MSH6: INTACT nuclear expression (no loss of expression)
  - PMS2: INTACT nuclear expression (no loss of expression)
  - Interpretation: MISMATCH REPAIR PROFICIENT (pMMR). Findings are CONSISTENT WITH MICROSATELLITE STABLE (MSS) tumor. No evidence of MMR deficiency by IHC.
  - Internal positive controls: satisfactory.

Tumor Origin Panel (Part B -- Hepatic Core Biopsy):
  - CK20 (Cytokeratin 20): POSITIVE (strong, diffuse cytoplasmic staining)
  - CDX2: POSITIVE (strong, diffuse nuclear staining)
  - CK7 (Cytokeratin 7): NEGATIVE (no staining in tumor cells)
  - TTF-1: NEGATIVE
  - Interpretation: Immunophenotype is CONSISTENT WITH METASTATIC COLORECTAL ADENOCARCINOMA (CK20+/CDX2+/CK7- pattern). Primary pulmonary, upper gastrointestinal, and pancreatobiliary origins are not supported.

Additional Markers (Part A):
  - CDX2: POSITIVE (strong, diffuse nuclear staining) -- supports colonic origin
  - CK20: POSITIVE (strong, diffuse cytoplasmic staining)
  - CK7: NEGATIVE

---

MOLECULAR / MUTATIONAL ANALYSIS (Part A -- Sigmoid Colon Mass):

RAS/RAF Mutation Testing (PCR-based, reflexed per institutional protocol for colorectal adenocarcinoma):

  KRAS Exon 2 (Codons 12/13):
    - KRAS codon 12 mutation DETECTED: p.G12D (c.35G>A)
    - KRAS codon 13: wild-type
    - Interpretation: KRAS codon 12 activating mutation identified. This mutation is associated with resistance to anti-EGFR monoclonal antibody therapy (cetuximab, panitumumab). Anti-EGFR agents are NOT recommended.

  KRAS Exon 3 (Codon 61): Wild-type
  KRAS Exon 4 (Codons 117/146): Wild-type

  NRAS Exon 2 (Codons 12/13): Wild-type
  NRAS Exon 3 (Codon 61): Wild-type
  NRAS Exon 4 (Codons 117/146): Wild-type
  Interpretation: NRAS: WILD-TYPE (no extended RAS mutations detected)

  BRAF V600E (Allele-Specific PCR, Cobas):
    - BRAF V600E: NOT DETECTED (wild-type)
    - Interpretation: BRAF V600E-negative. BRAF-targeted therapy (encorafenib) with anti-EGFR doublet not applicable in the first-line setting on this basis.

---

ANCILLARY STUDIES -- PENDING:

The following studies have been ordered and results are PENDING at the time of this report. An addendum will be issued upon completion:

  1. COMPREHENSIVE GENOMIC PROFILING (CGP) by Next-Generation Sequencing (NGS) -- submitted from Part B (hepatic core biopsy, higher tumor cellularity cores): Pending. Expected reporting: 10-14 business days. Panel covers 324 cancer-related genes including copy number alterations, gene fusions/rearrangements, and tumor mutational burden (TMB). This will include extended RAS/RAF, HER2/ERBB2 amplification status, PIK3CA, PTEN, TP53, APC, SMAD4, and other relevant genes. MSI status by NGS will also be reported.

  2. HER2 (ERBB2) IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY and/or FISH -- Part A (Sigmoid Colon): Pending. To be performed per updated colorectal cancer institutional reflex protocol given advanced/metastatic disease at presentation.

  3. GERMLINE MISMATCH REPAIR (MMR) GENE PANEL -- Blood specimen ordered (submitted separately by oncology). Results will be reported separately under molecular pathology accession. Includes MLH1, MSH2, MSH6, PMS2, EPCAM, and associated Lynch syndrome genes.

---

DIAGNOSIS:

Part A -- SIGMOID COLON MASS, COLONOSCOPIC BIOPSY:
  ADENOCARCINOMA, MODERATELY DIFFERENTIATED (Grade 2)
  - Irregular malignant glandular structures with intraluminal dirty necrosis
  - Desmoplastic stromal reaction
  - Submucosal invasion present
  - MMR STATUS: MISMATCH REPAIR PROFICIENT (pMMR / MSS) -- MLH1, MSH2, MSH6, PMS2 all show intact nuclear expression
  - KRAS: MUTANT (p.G12D, c.35G>A) -- anti-EGFR therapy contraindicated
  - NRAS: WILD-TYPE (exons 2-4)
  - BRAF V600E: NOT DETECTED
  - HER2 IHC/FISH: PENDING (see addendum)
  - NGS comprehensive profiling: PENDING (see addendum)

Part B -- HEPATIC SEGMENT VII LESION, CT-GUIDED CORE NEEDLE BIOPSY:
  METASTATIC ADENOCARCINOMA, CONSISTENT WITH COLORECTAL PRIMARY
  - Histomorphology and immunophenotype (CK20+, CDX2+, CK7-, TTF-1-) consistent with colorectal primary
  - Moderately differentiated (Grade 2), identical morphology to Part A
  - Background hepatic parenchyma with mild steatosis; no significant fibrosis on limited sampling
  - Additional molecular profiling (CGP/NGS): PENDING (see addendum)

---

COMMENT:
The combined findings of Parts A and B confirm a diagnosis of moderately differentiated colorectal adenocarcinoma of the sigmoid colon with synchronous hepatic metastasis to segment VII. The immunophenotype of the hepatic lesion (CK20+/CDX2+/CK7-) is classic for colorectal origin and excludes other primary sites in the differential including lung adenocarcinoma, pancreaticobiliary adenocarcinoma, and cholangiocarcinoma. The intact expression of all four MMR proteins by IHC is consistent with a microsatellite stable (MSS/pMMR) tumor. MSS colorectal cancers represent approximately 85% of metastatic colorectal cancers and are associated with a lower probability of response to immune checkpoint inhibitor monotherapy. The identified KRAS p.G12D mutation is the most common KRAS alteration in colorectal cancer (accounting for approximately 40% of all KRAS-mutant CRC) and is associated with primary resistance to anti-EGFR monoclonal antibody therapy. Comprehensive genomic profiling on the hepatic core biopsy (highest anticipated tumor cellularity) is pending and will provide additional clinically actionable information including HER2 amplification status, extended mutation profiling, and tumor mutational burden.

Results communicated to the ordering physician team. Correlation with clinical and radiologic findings recommended.

---

SIGNED:
Attending Pathologist: [REDACTED], M.D.
Department of Pathology and Laboratory Medicine
Gastrointestinal and Hepatobiliary Pathology Section

Report Status: PRELIMINARY (Addendum pending for NGS/CGP and HER2 results)

---

[ADDENDUM -- ISSUED SUBSEQUENTLY]

ADDENDUM TO SURGICAL PATHOLOGY REPORT -- Accession SP-[REDACTED]

Re: Parts A and B -- Sigmoid Colon Adenocarcinoma / Hepatic Metastasis

The following previously pending results are now available:

HER2 (ERBB2) IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY -- Part A (Sigmoid Colon Mass):
  - HER2 IHC score: 1+ (weak, incomplete basolateral staining in less than 10% of tumor cells)
  - Interpretation: NEGATIVE for HER2 overexpression by IHC (score 1+; does not meet criteria for FISH reflex testing)
  - HER2 status: NON-AMPLIFIED / NEGATIVE by IHC

COMPREHENSIVE GENOMIC PROFILING (NGS, 324-gene panel) -- Part B (Hepatic Core Biopsy):
  Results reported under separate Molecular Pathology Accession. See NGS report for full details.
  Summary of key findings:
  - KRAS p.G12D confirmed (VAF 42%)
  - TP53 p.R248W (VAF 51%)
  - APC p.R1450* (VAF 48%)
  - SMAD4 p.R361H (VAF 19%) -- associated with adverse prognosis in metastatic CRC
  - PIK3CA p.E545K (VAF 18%)
  - FBXW7 p.R465H (VAF 22%)
  - CDK8 amplification, MYC amplification, PTEN homozygous deletion
  - Tumor Mutational Burden (TMB): 8 mutations/Mb (LOW)
  - Microsatellite Status by NGS: MICROSATELLITE STABLE (MSS)
  - HER2 (ERBB2): NOT AMPLIFIED (copy number 2)
  - No actionable fusions detected (ALK, RET, NTRK: negative)

ADDENDUM FINAL DIAGNOSIS -- Revised:

Part A -- SIGMOID COLON MASS, COLONOSCOPIC BIOPSY:
  ADENOCARCINOMA, MODERATELY DIFFERENTIATED (Grade 2) -- FINAL
  - pMMR (MSS): MLH1/MSH2/MSH6/PMS2 intact
  - KRAS: MUTANT (p.G12D)
  - NRAS: WILD-TYPE
  - BRAF V600E: NOT DETECTED
  - HER2: NEGATIVE (IHC 1+, non-amplified)

Part B -- HEPATIC SEGMENT VII LESION, CT-GUIDED CORE NEEDLE BIOPSY:
  METASTATIC ADENOCARCINOMA, COLORECTAL PRIMARY -- FINAL
  - CK20+/CDX2+/CK7-/TTF-1-
  - Moderately differentiated, Grade 2
  - MSS by NGS; TMB low (8 mut/Mb)
  - KRAS G12D, TP53, APC, SMAD4, PIK3CA, FBXW7 mutations identified (see NGS report)
  - ERBB2: not amplified

Addendum Signed:
Attending Pathologist: [REDACTED], M.D.
Molecular Pathology Co-Attestation: [REDACTED], Ph.D.
Report Status: FINAL
